---
# ============================================================
# LATEX BACKEND — produnis/quarto-cv  (moderncv "classic")
# Install once: quarto use template produnis/quarto-cv
# Render:       quarto render cv.qmd
# Output:       cv.pdf  cv.html  cv.md
#
# moderncv "classic" style is visually close to the current
# nih.cls formatting: left-aligned section headers, name at
# top, contact line underneath.
# ============================================================
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
author: "John Ehrlinger, PhD"

format:
  quarto-cv-pdf:
    keep-tex: false
    lang: en
    date-format: "MMMM YYYY"
    biblatexoptions:
      - style=authoryear
      - backend=biber
      - sorting=ydnt
      - maxbibnames=99
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
  gfm:
    output-file: JohnEhrlinger-CV.md

moderncvtheme: classic   # classic | casual
cvoption: blue           # blue | orange | green | red | grey

# moderncv header fields (template uses German variable names)
vorname: "John"
nachname: "Ehrlinger, PhD"
graduate: "Assistant Staff, Lead Data Scientist"
strasse: ""
plz: ""
ort: "Hiram, Ohio"
telefon: ""

# Contact block (classic style renders these in header)
address: "Hiram, Ohio"
phone: ~                 # omit if not wanted
email: "ehrlinj@ccf.org"
homepage: "ehrlinger.github.io"
github: "ehrlinger"
linkedin: "ehrlinger"
orcid: "0000-0002-5340-5154"

# Bibliography — same .bib files as current LaTeX CV
bibliography:
  - cv-primary.bib
  - submitted.bib
  - inprep.bib
  - technotes.bib
  - talks.bib

# keyword → section heading mapping (matches existing bib keywords)
subbibliography: true
publikationen:
  - list:
      key: primary
      title: "Publications"
  - list:
      key: secondary
      title: "In Revision / Submitted"
  - list:
      key: inprep
      title: "In Preparation"
  - list:
      key: technote
      title: "Technical Reports"
  - list:
      key: presentation
      title: "Conference Presentations"

# Author highlighting in bibliography
# Add  author+an = {1=highlight}  to .bib entries where Ehrlinger is author 1
# or use the biblatex myname command from existing CV
---

## Research Interests

Applied statistical machine learning research conducted in close collaboration with
cardiovascular surgeons and clinicians. Methodological focus spans random forest and
ensemble methods, clustering, deep learning, and time series analysis, with emphasis
on time-to-event and longitudinal data in cardiovascular outcomes. A sustained focus
is the translation of methodological advances into clinical practice through open-source
software development and reproducible analytical workflows.

## Professional Experience

```{=latex}
\cventry{Dec 2024--Present}{Assistant Staff, Lead Data Scientist}{Cleveland Clinic}{Cleveland, OH}{}{
  Cardiovascular Outcomes, Registries and Research (CORR) ---
  Heart, Vascular \& Thoracic Institute.
  Lead a team of data engineers and data scientists supporting cardiovascular outcomes
  research within CORR. Drive statistical methods research as applied to observational
  clinical research. Champion software engineering best practices and implement process
  improvements to optimize the departmental research pipeline.
}

\cventry{Jun 2025--Present}{Clinical Assistant Professor \textnormal{(Joint Appointment)}}{Cleveland Clinic Lerner College of Medicine}{Cleveland, OH}{}{
  Serving as faculty mentor and statistical advisor to medical students and trainees
  conducting clinical research within the Heart, Vascular \& Thoracic Institute.
  Providing guidance in statistical methods, research design, and data analysis
  for trainee-led research projects.
}

\cventry{Mar 2023--Oct 2024}{Senior Data Scientist -- Technical Lead}{Altamira Technologies}{McLean, VA}{}{
  Technical lead of data science team supporting USAF customers on secure networks.
  Built reusable Plotly-based dashboard framework; developed predictive traffic flow
  models and computer vision pipeline for vehicle volume estimation at base entry points.
}

\cventry{Jul 2015--Apr 2023}{Senior Data and Applied Scientist -- Technical Lead}{Microsoft}{Cambridge, MA}{}{
  Azure Global Commercial Industry (AGCI-AI). Technical lead for ML/AI customer
  engagements across oil \& gas (Chevron), aerospace (Rolls Royce), and medical devices
  (Stryker). Developed published Azure ML solution accelerators for predictive
  maintenance. Presented at MLADS conference four times (2016--2020).
}

\newpage

\cventry{Aug 2012--Jul 2015}{Assistant Staff / Assistant Professor of Medicine}{Cleveland Clinic}{Cleveland, OH}{}{
  Department of Quantitative Health Sciences -- Lerner Research Institute.
  Applied random survival forests to cardiovascular outcomes research.
  Initiated development of the ggRandomForests R package.
}

\cventry{2015}{Clinical Assistant Professor \textnormal{(Joint Appointment)}}{Cleveland Clinic Lerner College of Medicine}{Cleveland, OH}{}{
  Mentored medical students and fellows in statistical methods and research design
  as part of joint fellowship programs within the Cardiovascular Outcomes,
  Registries and Research (CORR) group.
}

\cventry{Aug 1999--Aug 2012}{Lead Systems Analyst: Scientific Programmer}{Cleveland Clinic}{Cleveland, OH}{}{
  Department of Thoracic and Cardiovascular Surgery -- Heart and Vascular Institute.
  Supported research computing infrastructure, statistical software development,
  and HPC operations. Completed doctoral research in Statistics at CWRU during this period.
}
```

## Education

```{=latex}
\cventry{2011}{Doctor of Philosophy (PhD)}{Case Western Reserve University}{Cleveland, OH}{Statistics}{
  Dissertation: \textit{Regularization: Stagewise Regression and Bagging} ---
  Advisor: Hemant Ishwaran
}
\cventry{1994}{Master of Science (MS)}{Case Western Reserve University}{Cleveland, OH}{Mechanical Engineering / Aerospace Engineering}{
  Thesis: \textit{A comparison of empirical axial flow compressor models} ---
  Advisor: Frances E.\ McCaughan
}
\cventry{1993}{Bachelor of Science (BS)}{Case Western Reserve University}{Cleveland, OH}{Mechanical Engineering}{}
```

## Software

**R Packages**

- *ggRandomForests* — Visual exploration of random forest models. <https://github.com/ehrlinger/ggRandomForests>
- *boostmtree* — Boosted multivariate trees for longitudinal data. <https://github.com/ehrlinger/boostmtree>
- *mixhazard* — R port of the C code underlying the Cleveland Clinic Hazard SAS module. <https://github.com/ehrlinger/mixhazard>
- *hvtiPlotR* — Publication-quality HVTI graphics. <https://github.com/ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR>
- *hvtiRutilities* — Reproducible research utilities. <https://github.com/ehrlinger/hvtiRutilities>

**SAS/C Software**

- *hazard* — SAS and C implementation of multi-phase hazard analysis for time-to-event decomposition. Includes C source code, SAS macros and modules. (Maintainer) <https://github.com/ehrlinger/hazard>

## Honors & Awards

- NASA Graduate Research Fellowship · 1992–1994
- Cleveland Clinic Innovations Award · 2003
